stop.__description: Get or set the ending two-dimensional coordinates of the linear gradient
stop.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("LinearGradient.stop sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

local gradient = canvas:LinearGradient { [0] = 0xE30940FF, [0.33] = 0xE7D702FF, [0.66] = 0x0FA895FF, [1] = 0x1373E8FF }
gradient.start = { 0, 0 }
gradient.stop = { canvas.width, 0 }

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:fillrect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height, gradient)
end

ui.run(win):wait()

opacity.__description: Get or set the degree of opacity of the linear gradient, a number starting from 0.0(full transparency) to 1.0(full opacity)
opacity.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("LinearGradient.opacity sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

local gradient = canvas:LinearGradient { [0] = 0xE30940FF, [0.33] = 0xE7D702FF, [0.66] = 0x0FA895FF, [1] = 0x1373E8FF }
gradient.start = { 0, 0 }
gradient.stop = { canvas.width, 0 }
gradient.opacity = 0.5

function canvas:onPaint()
  self.begin()
  self:clear()
  self:fillrect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height, gradient)
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()
opacity.__tinydesc: Get/set the LinearGradient degree of opacity

start.__description: Get or set the starting two-dimensional coordinates of the linear gradient
start.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("LinearGradient.start sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

local gradient = canvas:LinearGradient { [0] = 0x00FFFFFF, [1] = 0x00008bFF }
gradient.start = { 0, 0 }
gradient.stop = { canvas.width, canvas.height }

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:fillrect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height, gradient)
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

LinearGradient.__description: The Canvas.LinearGradient object draws a smooth color blend along a line
LinearGradient.__tinydesc: Object representation of a linear colored gradient

center.__description: Get or set the center coordinates of the gradient ellipse
center.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("RadialGradient.center sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

local gradient = canvas:RadialGradient { [0] = 0x00FFFFFF, [1] = 0x00008bFF }
gradient.center = { canvas.width/2, canvas.height/2 }
gradient.radius = { canvas.width/2, canvas.height/2 }

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:fillrect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height, gradient)
end

function canvas:onHover(x,y buttons)
  gradient.center = { x, y }
end

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
  ui.update()
until not win.visible

radius.__description: Get or set the horizontal and vertical radius of the gradient ellipse
radius.__tinydesc: Get/set the RadialGradient horizontal and vertical radius

RadialGradient.__description: The Canvas.RadialGradient object draws a smooth color blend radiating from a center point

opacity.__description: Get or set the degree of opacity of the radial gradient, a number starting from 0.0(full transparency) to 1.0(full opacity)
opacity.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("RadialGradient.opacity sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

local gradient = canvas:RadialGradient { [0] = 0xE30940FF, [0.33] = 0xE7D702FF, [0.66] = 0x0FA895FF, [1] = 0x1373E8FF }
gradient.center = { canvas.width/2, canvas.height/2 }
gradient.radius = { canvas.width/2, canvas.height/2 }
gradient.opacity = 0

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear()
  self:fillrect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height, gradient)
  self:flip()
  gradient.opacity = gradient.opacity+0.005
end

ui.run(win):wait()
opacity.__tinydesc: Get/set the RadialGradient degree of opacity

RadialGradient.__tinydesc: Object representation of a radial colored gradient

draw.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Image:draw() sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.bgcolor = 0x000000FF

local w = canvas.width
local h = canvas.height
local img = canvas:Image("LuaRT.png")

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  img:draw(math.random(w)-80, math.random(h), math.random(), math.random())
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the horizontal position (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the vertical position (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
An optional number that represent a ratio to apply to to the Image width and height. The default value is 1, meaning that the original ratio will be used
An optional number representing the degree of opacity, defaulting to 1 (full opacity). A value of 0.5 means middle transparency

An optional string used to specify the quality of image scaling :

  • "nearest" : Use the exact color of the nearest bitmap pixel to the current rendering pixel (the default)./li>
  • "linear" : Uses a four point sample and linear interpolation, using more processing time, but outputs a higher quality image.

draw.__description: Draws the Image on its Canvas, at the specified position
draw.__tinydesc: Draws the Image on the Canvas

width.__description: Get the Image width. The width starts from 0 (left side of the Image) and increases to the right direction.
width.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Image.Image() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
local img = canvas:Image("LuaRT.png")
local xpos = -img.width

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear()
  img:draw(xpos, 30)
  xpos = xpos > canvas.width and -img.width or xpos+6
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

drawsub.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Image:drawsub sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.bgcolor = 0x000000FF

local w = canvas.width
local h = canvas.height
local img = canvas:Image("LuaRT.png")

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  img:drawsub(math.random(w), math.random(h), math.random(500), math.random(400), math.random(), math.random())
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the horizontal position (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the vertical position (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
A number that represents upper left corner x position of the sub part in the Image.
A number that represents upper left corner y position of the sub part in the Image.
A number that represents the width of the sub part in the Image.
A number that represents the height of the sub part in the Image.
An optional number representing the degree of opacity, defaulting to 1 (full opacity). A value of 0.5 means middle transparency

An optional string used to specify the quality of image scaling :

  • "nearest" : Use the exact color of the nearest bitmap pixel to the current rendering pixel (the default).
  • "linear" : Uses a four point sample and linear interpolation, using more processing time, but outputs a higher quality image.

drawsub.__description: Draws a sub part of the Image on the Canvas.
drawsub.__tinydesc: Draws a sub part of the Image on the Canvas

drawrect.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Image:drawrect sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.bgcolor = 0x000000FF

local w = canvas.width
local h = canvas.height
local img = canvas:Image("LuaRT.png")

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  img:drawrect(math.random(w), math.random(h), math.random(500), math.random(400), math.random(), math.random())
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the horizontal position (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the vertical position (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
A number that represent the Image width
A number that represent the Image height
An optional number representing the degree of opacity, defaulting to 1 (full opacity). A value of 0.5 means middle transparency

An optional string used to specify the quality of image scaling :

  • "nearest" : Use the exact color of the nearest bitmap pixel to the current rendering pixel (the default).
  • "linear" : Uses a four point sample and linear interpolation, using more processing time, but outputs a higher quality image.

drawrect.__description: Draws the Image on its Canvas, using the specified position, width, height and opacity.
drawrect.__tinydesc: Draws the Image resized on the Canvas

Image.__description: The Canvas.Image object loads and draws an image file onto a canvas surface

height.__description: Get the height of the Image. The height starts from 0 (top side of the Image) and increase to the bottom direction.
height.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Image.height sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
local img = canvas:Image("LuaRT.png")
local ypos = -img.height

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear()
  img:draw(-60, ypos, 0.9)
  ypos = ypos > canvas.height and -img.height or ypos+3
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

Image.__tinydesc: Object representation of a picture to be drawn on a Canvas

__description: This module provides a drawing surface as a Canvas widget in the ui namespace

onClick.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onClick() event sample", "fixed", 320, 200)
win:status("Click on the Canvas to change its color")

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 0, 100, 100)
canvas:center()
canvas.cursor = "hand"

function canvas:onClick()
  self.bgcolor = math.random(0xFFFFFF)
end

canvas:onClick()

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Canvas area.

The vertical position of the mouse in the Canvas area.

Notes

  • This function provides DIP (device independent pixels) coordinates.

onClick.__description: This event is fired when the user has clicked on the Canvas.
onClick.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user clicks on the Canvas

Canvas.__tinydesc: Object representation of a drawing surface

fontsize.__description: Get or set the current Canvas font size, a number, expressed "Device-Independent Pixel" units. A DIP equals 1/96 inch.
fontsize.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:align() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)

-- align the Canvas in all of its parent area 
canvas.font = "Arial"
canvas.fontsize = 18

-- don't use onPaint() event, draw only once
for i=1, 9 do
  canvas.fontsize = i*5
  canvas:print(canvas.font, 6, i*17, math.random(0xFFFFFFFF))
end

ui.run(win):wait()
fontsize.__tinydesc: Get/set the canvas current font size

width.__description: Get or set the Canvas area width. The width starts from 0 (left side of the Canvas) and increases to the right direction.
width.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.height sample", 600, 400)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
Canvas.align = "all"

function Canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear() 
  self:fillellipse(300,200, 50, 50, 0xdc4f2bff)
  self:flip()
end

-- Launch async Task for ui
ui.run(win)

-- Async Task to decrease Canvas height
async(function()
    while win.visible do
        Canvas.width = Canvas.width - 5
        if Canvas.width <= 0 then
            win:hide()
            sys.exit()
        end
        sleep()
    end
end)

-- wait for all tasks
waitall()

show.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onShow() sample", 640, 480)

-- create a canvas and center it
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 0, 600, 400)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.visible = false

-- set the onShow() event handler to draw on the canvas
function canvas:onShow()
    self:begin()
    -- clears the canvas backbuffer with white color
    self:clear(0xFFFFFFFF)
    -- prints text
    self:print("Canvas is now visible !", 10, 10)
    -- shows the backbuffer
    self:flip()
end

-- Launch a Task to show the Canvas in 2sec
async(function()
    sleep(2000)
    canvas:show()
end)

ui.run(win):wait()
show.__description: Show and activate the Canvas (events can now be fired).

measure.__returns: This function returns the text size as a table with two numeric fields: width and height
measure.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.measure() sample - Move the mouse over the window", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.font = "Impact"
canvas.fontsize = 40

-- create a RadialGradient 
local radial = canvas:RadialGradient { [0] = 0x00FFFFFF, [1] = 0x00008BFF }
radial.radius = { 400, 400 }

local text = "Hello LuaRT World !!!"

-- center the text
local size = canvas:measure(text)
local xpos = (canvas.width - size.width)/2
local ypos = (canvas.height - size.height)/2

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear() 
  self:print(text, xpos, ypos, radial)
  self:flip()
end

function canvas:onHover(x, y, buttons)
  radial.center = { x, y }
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A string representing the text to be measured

measure.__tinydesc: Measures the text size on the Canvas

onMouseWheel.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Canvas - onMouseWheel event example", "fixed", 320, 240)

local c = ui.Canvas(win)
c.align = "all"

local width = c.width/2
local height = c.height/2
local scale = 1

function c:onMouseWheel(delta)
    scale = scale + 0.05*delta/120 
end

function c:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear()
  c:identity()
  c:scale(scale, scale)
  c:fillrect(width-35, height-35, width+35, height+35, 0xA569BDFF)
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description:
A numberthat indicates the distance the wheel is rotated, expressed in multiples or divisions of 120.
A positive value indicates that the wheel was rotated forward, away from the user, a negative value indicates that the wheel was rotated backward, toward the user.

A table which fields indicates if mouse buttons or special keys have been pressed during the mouse up event :

  • "left": true if left mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "right": true if right mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "middle": true if middle mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "control": true if the CONTROL key is pressed, false otherwise
  • "shift": true if the SHIFT mouse button is pressed, false otherwise

onMouseWheel.__description: This event is fired when the user rotates the mouse wheel while beeing over the Canvas.
onMouseWheel.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user rotates the mouse wheel

parent.__results: Window | Groupbox | TabItem
parent.__description: Get the parent object that owns the Canvas.
parent.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Canvas 
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.parent sample", 640, 480)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 50, 640, 500)
Canvas:clear(0xd5a0f8)

local button = ui.Button(win, "Click to show Canvas.parent")
button:center()
button.y = 10

function button:onClick()
  ui.info("Canvas parent is "..tostring(Canvas.parent))
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: An object that will own the Canvas. Parent objects can be any of Window, Groupbox and TabItem
An optional number that indicates the Canvas horizontal position, in pixels. Zero means the left border of the parent.
An optional number that indicates the Canvas vertical position, in pixels. Zero means the top border of the parent.
An optional number that indicates the Canvas width in pixels, autosized to fit text content if omitted.
An optional number that indicates the Canvas height in pixels, autosized to fit text content if omitted.

constructor.__description: The Canvas constructor returns a Canvas instance representing a drawing surface.
constructor.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.enabled sample", 320, 200)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
Canvas.align = "all"

function Canvas:onClick(x, y)
  ui.info("You have clicked on the Canvas at position "..x..", "..y)
end

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
	ui.update()
until not win.visible

height.__description: Get or set the height of the Canvas area. The height starts from 0 (top side of the Canvas) and increase to the bottom direction.
height.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.height sample", 600, 400)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
Canvas.align = "all"

function Canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear() 
  self:fillellipse(300,200, 50, 50, 0xdc4f2bff)
  self:flip()
end

-- Launch async Task for ui
ui.run(win)

-- Async Task to decrease Canvas height
async(function()
    while win.visible do
        Canvas.height = Canvas.height - 5
        if Canvas.height <= 0 then
            win:hide()

        end

-- wait for all tasks

fillcircle.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.fillcircle() sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.bgcolor = 0x000000FF

local w = canvas.width
local h = canvas.height


function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  local radial = self:RadialGradient { [0] = math.random(0xFFFFFFFF), [1] = math.random(0xFFFFFFFF) }
  radial.radius = { 400, 400 }
  self:fillcircle(math.random(w), math.random(h), math.random(150), radial)
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the x coordinate of the upper left corner (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the y coordinate of the upper left corner (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
A number representing the radius of the fillcircle

An optional value that can represent any of the following values :

  • A RGBA color, as an integer value
  • LinearGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored linear gradient
  • RadialGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored radial gradient
  • Image : The line will be drawn with a picture pattern.
When this parameter is omitted, the rectangle is drawn using the current Canvas.color

fillcircle.__description: Draws a filled circle on the Canvas

skew.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Canvas - Skew example", "fixed", 150, 150)

local c = ui.Canvas(win)
c.align = "all"
c.color = 0xd01000ff

local skew = 0

-- Uses internal timer for 30fps drawing
function c:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear(0xffffffff)
  c:identity()
  c:skew(skew, 0)
  c:fillrect(50,50,100,100, 0x37A0D7)
  skew = skew + 0.1
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the x-axis skew angle in degrees.
A number representing the y-axis skew angle in degrees.
The horizonal coordinate for the skew center point, defaulting to the center of the Canvas.
The vertical coordinate for the skew center point, defaulting to the center of the Canvas.

skew.__description: Applyies a skew transformation on the Canvas

onMouseDown.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onMouseDown() event sample", "fixed", 320, 200)
win:status("")

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 0, 100, 100)
canvas.cursor = "cross"
canvas.bgcolor = 0xFFA0A0FF
canvas:center()

function canvas:onMouseDown(x, y, buttons)
  local btn = ""
  if buttons.left then btn = btn.." left" end
  if buttons.right then btn = btn.." right" end
  if buttons.middle then btn = btn.." middle" end
  if buttons.shift then btn = btn.." shift" end
  win:status("x : "..math.floor(x), "y : "..math.floor(y), "buttons : "..(btn == "" and "none" or btn))
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Canvas area.

The vertical position of the mouse in the Canvas area.

Notes

  • This function provides DIP (device independent pixels) coordinates.

A table which fields indicates if mouse buttons or special keys have been pressed during the mouse down event :

  • "left": true if left mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "right": true if right mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "middle": true if middle mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "control": true if the CONTROL key is pressed, false otherwise
  • "shift": true if the SHIFT mouse button is pressed, false otherwise

onMouseDown.__description: This event is fired when the user press a mouse button while beeing over the Canvas.
onMouseDown.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user presses a mouse button over the Canvas

flip.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.flip() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

local x = 160
local y = 100

function win:onKey(key)
  if key == "VK_UP" then
    y = y - 4
  elseif key == "VK_DOWN" then
    y = y + 4
  elseif key == "VK_LEFT" then
    x = x - 4
  elseif key == "VK_RIGHT" then
    x = x + 4
  end
end

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear()
  self:fillcircle(x, y, 5, 0xA0D0A0)
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()
flip.__description: Flips the back buffer, displaying it on the Canvas.
flip.__tinydesc: Flips the back buffer and display it

capture.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Capture demo", 320, 130)
local c = ui.Canvas(win)
c.align = "all"

function c:onPaint()
    self:begin()
    self:clear(0xFFFFFFFF)                      
    self:fillrect(10, 10, 310, 120, 0xFF0078D7)
    self:print("Hello LuaRT", 130, 60, 0xFFFFFFFF)
    self:flip()
end

win:show()

-- wait 1s...
sleep(1000)

--.. then capture the canvas content
c:capture("capture.png")

-- keep window visible briefly then exit
sleep(800)

__params.__description:
A File or a filename as a string.
The image format is determined by the file extension, which may be one of the following values : .bmp, .png, .jpg|.jpeg, .bmp, .tiff|.tif and .gif.

capture.__description: Captures the current canvas content and saves it as a file on the disk.

onCreate.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- creates a simple window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onCreate() event sample", 640, 400)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 320, 200)

-- Canvas:onCreate() event to center and draw text on the Canvas widget
function Canvas:onCreate()
  self:begin()
  self:center()
  self:clear(0x000000FF)
  self:print("Hello LuaRT !", 130, 100, 0xFFA300FF)
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()
onCreate.__description:
This event is fired when the Canvas object has just been created (just after the Canvas:constructor() call).
This event is particularly interesting when you want to initialize its properties.
onCreate.__tinydesc: Event fired when Canvas has just been created

fontstyle.__description:
Get or set the current Canvas font style, a string value, which may be one of the following values :
  • "normal" : The characters in a normal, or roman, font are upright.
  • "italic" : The characters in an italic font are truly slanted and appear as they were designed.
  • "oblique" : The characters in an oblique font are artificially slanted.
fontstyle.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:fontstyle() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)

-- align the Canvas in all of its parent area 
canvas.font = "Times New Roman"
canvas.fontsize = 37.5
canvas.fontstyle = "italic"

-- don't use onPaint() event, draw only once
canvas:print(canvas.font, 16, 70)

ui.run(win):wait()
fontstyle.__tinydesc: Get/set the canvas current font style

visible.__description:
Get or set the Canvas visibility on screen, a true value means that the Canvas is shown, a false value means that the Canvas is hidden.
The Canvas.visible property is also affected by the Canvas:show() and Canvas:hide() methods.
visible.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Canvas 
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.visible sample", "fixed", 640, 480)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 50, 640, 430)
Canvas.fontsize = 30

function Canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear() 
  self:print("Hello", math.random(0,630), math.random(0, 390), math.random(0xFFFFFFFF))
  self:flip()
end

local button = ui.Button(win, "Hide the Canvas widget")
button:center()
button.y = 10

function button:onClick()
  Canvas.visible = not Canvas.visible
  button.text = (Canvas.visible and "Hide" or "Show").. " the Canvas widget"
end

ui.run(win):wait()
visible.__tinydesc: Controls whether the canvas appears on screen

print.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.print() sample - Move the mouse over the window", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.font = "Impact"
canvas.fontsize = 40

-- create a RadialGradient 
local radial = canvas:RadialGradient { [0] = 0x00FFFFFF, [1] = 0x00008BFF }
radial.radius = { 400, 400 }

local text = "Hello LuaRT World !!!"

-- center the text
local size = canvas:measure(text)
local xpos = (canvas.width - size.width)/2
local ypos = (canvas.height - size.height)/2

function canvas:onPaint()
  canvas:print(text, xpos, ypos, radial)
end

function canvas:onHover(x,y buttons)
  radial.center = { x, y }
end

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
  ui.update()
until not win.visible

__params.__description: A string representing the text to be drawn
The horizontal position in the Canvas as a number (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
The vertical position in the Canvas as a number (zero means the top side of the Canvas)

An optional value that can represent any of the following values :

  • A RGBA color, as an integer value
  • LinearGradient : The text will be filled with a colored linear gradient
  • RadialGradient : The text will be filled with a colored radial gradient
  • Image : The text will be filled with a picture pattern.
When this parameter is omitted, the text is drawn using the current Canvas.color

print.__description: Draws text on the Canvas, at the specified position

onContext.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onContext() event sample", "fixed", 320, 200)
win:status("Right click on the Canvas")

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 0, 100, 100)
canvas:center()
canvas.bgcolor = 0xFF0000FF

function canvas:onContext()
  win:popup(ui.Menu("Item 1", "Item 2", "Item 3"))
end

ui.run(win):wait()
onContext.__description: This event is fired when the user has clicked on the Canvas with the right mouse button.
onContext.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user right click on the Canvas

onPaint.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onPait() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

-- set the onPaint() event handler to draw on the canvas
function canvas:onPaint()
    self:begin()
    self:clear()
    self:print("Hello World !", 10, 10)
    self:flip()
end

-- shows the Window and wait for it to be closed
ui.run(win):wait()
onPaint.__description: This event is fired continuously to redraw the Canvas. You can put all your drawing operations inside this event handler function.
onPaint.__tinydesc: Event fired when canvas need to be redrawn

onShow.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onShow() sample", 640, 480)

-- create a canvas and center it
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 0, 600, 400)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.visible = false

-- set the onShow() event handler to draw on the canvas
function canvas:onShow()
    self:begin()
    -- clears the canvas backbuffer with white color
    self:clear(0xFFFFFFFF)
    -- prints text
    self:print("Canvas is now visible !", 10, 10)
    -- shows the backbuffer
    self:flip()
end

-- Launch a Task to show the Canvas in 2sec
async(function()
    sleep(2000)
    canvas:show()
end)

ui.run(win):wait()
onShow.__description: This event is fired when the Canvas is shown (with a call to Canvas:show() or setting the Canvas.visible property to true).
onShow.__tinydesc: Event fired when canvas is shown

identity.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Canvas - Scaling example", "fixed", 150, 150)

local c = ui.Canvas(win)
c.align = "all"

local scale = 0
local delta = 0.02

-- Uses internal timer for 30fps drawing
function c:onPaint()
    self:begin()
    self:clear(0xffffffff)
    -- Resets the canvas transformations
    c:identity()
    -- Sets the new scale transformation
    c:scale(scale, scale)
    c:fillrect(50,50,100,100, 0xF0A0D7FF)
    self:flip()
    sleep()
    scale = scale + delta
    if scale > 2 then
        delta = -0.02
    elseif scale < 0.02 then
        delta = 0.02
    end
end

ui.run(win):wait()
identity.__description: Resets the Canvas transformation to the default identity matrix

fontstretch.__description:
Get or set the current Canvas font stretch. A font's stretch factor refers to how much the font has been stretched when compared to its normal aspect ratio.

This property uses an integer value from 1 to 9 to specify the font stretch. Lower values indicate narrower widths, while higher values indicate wider widths.

Stretch values are not directly related to visual appearance, but rather to metadata that the current font may or may not provide.
fontstretch.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:align() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)

-- align the Canvas in all of its parent area 
canvas.font = "Arial"
canvas.fontsize = 18

-- don't use onPaint() event, draw only once
for i=1, 9 do
  canvas.fontstretch = i
  canvas:print(canvas.font, 6, i*17)
end

ui.run(win):wait()
fontstretch.__tinydesc: Get/set the canvas current font stretch

fontweight.__description: Get or set the current Canvas font size, an integer value between 1 and 999. A smaller value indicates a lighter weight, a larger value indicates a heavier weight.
fontweight.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:fontweight() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)

-- align the Canvas in all of its parent area 
canvas.font = "Times New Roman"
canvas.fontsize = 37.5
canvas.fontweight = 900

-- don't use onPaint() event, draw only once
canvas:print(canvas.font, 6, 70)

ui.run(win):wait()
fontweight.__tinydesc: Get/set the canvas current font weigth

onMouseUp.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onMouseUp() event sample", "fixed", 320, 200)
win:status("")

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 0, 100, 100)
canvas.cursor = "cross"
canvas.bgcolor = 0xFFA0A0FF
canvas:center()

function canvas:onMouseUp(x, y, buttons)
  local btn = ""
  if buttons.left then btn = btn.." left" end
  if buttons.right then btn = btn.." right" end
  if buttons.middle then btn = btn.." middle" end
  if buttons.shift then btn = btn.." shift" end
  win:status("x : "..math.floor(x), "y : "..math.floor(y), "buttons : "..(btn == "" and "none" or btn))
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Canvas area.

The vertical position of the mouse in the Canvas area.

Notes

  • This function provides DIP (device independent pixels) coordinates.

A table which fields indicates if mouse buttons or special keys have been pressed during the mouse up event :

  • "left": true if left mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "right": true if right mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "middle": true if middle mouse button is pressed, false otherwise
  • "control": true if the CONTROL key is pressed, false otherwise
  • "shift": true if the SHIFT mouse button is pressed, false otherwise

onMouseUp.__description: This event is fired when the user releases a mouse button while beeing over the Canvas.
onMouseUp.__tinydesc: Event fired when the user releases a mouse button over the Canvas

font.__description: Get or set the current Canvas font, a string representing a font family name, from the Windows system fonts.

center.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:center() sample", 640, 480)

-- create a canvas and center it
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 0, 600, 400)
canvas:center()

-- set the onPaint() event handler to draw on the canvas

  self:begin()

end

-- update the user interface asynchronously until the user closes the Window
ui.run(win):wait()
center.__description: Centers the Canvas on the parent widget.
center.__tinydesc: Centers the canvas on the parent widget

bgcolor.__description: Get or set the Canvas background color. When set, the canvas is cleared with this new color
bgcolor.__example:

require "canvas"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("canvas.bgcolor sample", "fixed", 500, 400)
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

win:show()

bgcolor.__tinydesc: Get/set the canvas actual background color

rotate.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Canvas - Rotation example", "fixed", 320, 240)

local c = ui.Canvas(win)
c.align = "all"

local degree = 0
local width = c.width/2
local height = c.height/2

function c:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear()
  c:rotate(degree)
  c:fillrect(width-35, height-35, width+35, height+35, 0xA569BDFF)
  degree = degree + 0.0005
  sleep()
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the rotation angle in degrees. A positive angle creates a clockwise rotation, and a negative angle creates a counterclockwise rotation.
The horizonal coordinate for the rotation center point, defaulting to the center of the Canvas.
The vertical coordinate for the rotation center point, defaulting to the center of the Canvas.

rotate.__description: Applyies a rotation transformation on the Canvas

clear.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.clear() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.sync = false

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
  canvas:begin()
  canvas:clear(math.random(0xFFFFFFFF))
  canvas:flip()
  ui.update()
  sleep(250)
until not win.visible

__params.__description: An optional interger representing a RGBA color value to clear and fill the Canvas with, or the current Canvas background color if omitted.

clear.__description: Clears the Canvas drawing surface with the current Canvas.bgcolor or with the provided RGBA color.
clear.__tinydesc: Clears the canvas surface and prepare it for drawing

translate.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Canvas - Translation example", "fixed", 320, 240)

local c = ui.Canvas(win)
c.align = "all"

local t = 0

function c:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear(0xffffffff)
  self:identity()
  self:translate(t, 0)
  self:fillrect(0, 90,50,140, 0x52BE80FF)
  t = t + 2
  if t > 300 then 
    t = -50
  end
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the translation along the x-axis.

translate.__description: Applyies a translation transformation on the Canvas

color.__description:
Get or set the Canvas drawing color.
  • The color is represented by a number, an RGBA value (one byte per primary color and one byte for alpha channel).
  • A RGBA color can be represented as an hexadecimal number : 0xRRGGBBAA , RR meaning a 8bit hexadecimal red value, and so on.
color.__example:
require "canvas"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("canvas.color sample", "fixed", 500, 400)
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas:clear(0x000000FF)

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
  canvas:begin()
  canvas.color = math.random(0xFFFFFFFF)
  for i=1,10 do
    canvas:fillellipse(math.random(500), math.random(400), math.random(15), math.random(15))
  end
  canvas:flip()
  ui.update()
until not win.visible
color.__tinydesc: Get/set the canvas actual color for drawing

RadialGradient.__returns: This function returns an RadialGradient instance.
RadialGradient.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("RadialGradient.constructor() sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

local logo = canvas:Image("LuaRT.png")

local gradient = canvas:RadialGradient { [0] = 0xffffe600, [1] = 0x000000FF }
gradient.radius = { canvas.width/4, canvas.height/4 }

function canvas:onPaint()
    self:begin()
    logo:draw(20, 100, 1, 1, "linear")
    self:fillrect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height, gradient)
    self:flip()
end

function canvas:onHover(x,y, buttons)
    gradient.center = { x, y }
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description:
A table where :

  • keys are a number : a relative gradient position (only positions between 0.0 to 1.0 are visible, but any other positions might impact the gradient).
  • values are a number : a color in RGBA format.

  • The color is represented by a number, an RGBA value (one byte per primary color and one byte for alpha channel).
  • A RGBA color can be represented as an hexadecimal number : 0xRRGGBBAA , RR meaning a 8bit hexadecimal red value, and so on.

RadialGradient.__description: Creates an RadialGradient object instance to be used in any Canvas drawing operation.
RadialGradient.__tinydesc: Creates a new radial gradient brush

__params.__description: The horizontal position of the mouse in the Canvas area.

The vertical position of the mouse in the Canvas area.

Notes

A table which fields indicates if mouse buttons or special keys have been pressed during the move :

  • "left": true if left mouse button is pressed, false otherwise

__params.__description: A number representing the horizontal position (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the vertical position (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
An optional integer that represent a RGBA color value, defaulting to the current Canvas.color

point.__description: Draws a point on the Canvas, at the specified position

Image.__returns: This function returns an Image instance.
Image.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.Image() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
local img = canvas:Image("LuaRT.png")
local xpos = -img.width

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:clear()
  img:draw(xpos, 30)
  xpos = xpos > canvas.width and -img.width or xpos+6
end

win:show()

-- update user interface
repeat
  ui.update()
until not win.visible

__params.__description:
A File or a filename as a string representing the file to load the image from.

The canvas module support the following file types :
  • BMP (Windows Bitmap Format) *.bmp
  • GIF (Graphics Interchange Format 89a) *.gif(not animated, first frame only)
  • ICO (Icon Format) *.ico
  • JPEG (Joint Photographic Experts Group) *.jpg | *.jpeg
  • PNG (Portable Network Graphics) *.png
  • TIFF (Tagged Image File Format) *.tiff | *.tif
  • Windows Media Photo *.wmp
  • DDS (DirectDraw Surface) *.dds

Image.__description: Creates an Image object instance, which content is loaded from a file.

line.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.line() sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.bgcolor = 0x000000FF

local w = canvas.width
local h = canvas.height

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:line(math.random(w), math.random(w), math.random(h), math.random(h), math.random(0xFFFFFFFF), 3)
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the horizontal position of the starting point (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the horizontal vertical of the starting point (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
A number representing the horizontal position of the ending point
A number representing the horizontal vertical of the ending point

An optional value that can represent any of the following values :

  • A RGBA color, as an integer value
  • LinearGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored linear gradient
  • RadialGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored radial gradient
  • Image : The line will be drawn with a picture pattern.
When this parameter is omitted, the line is drawn using the current Canvas.color
An optional number value representing the line thickness, defaulting to 1

__params.__description: A number representing the x coordinate of the upper left corner (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the y coordinate of the upper left corner (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
A number representing the x-radius of the ellipse
A number representing the y-radius of the ellipse

An optional value that can represent any of the following values :

  • A RGBA color, as an integer value
  • LinearGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored linear gradient
  • RadialGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored radial gradient
  • Image : The line will be drawn with a picture pattern.
When this parameter is omitted, the rectangle is drawn using the current Canvas.color
An optional number value representing the rectangle border thickness, defaulting to 1

onHide.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Canvas 
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onHide sample", "fixed", 640, 480)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 50, 640, 430)

ui.run(win):wait()
onHide.__description: This event is fired when the Canvas is hidden (with a call to Canvas:hide() or setting the Canvas.visible property to false).
onHide.__tinydesc: Event fired when canvas is hidden

x.__description: Get or set the Canvas horizontal position. The horizontal position start from 0 (left side of the parent widget) and increase to the right (right side of the parent widget).
x.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("canvas.x sample", 600, 300)
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 20)
ui.run(win)

async(function()
    while win.visible and (canvas.x < 600) do
        canvas.x = canvas.x + 2
        canvas:begin()
        canvas:clear(math.random(0xFFFFFFFF))
        canvas:flip()
        sleep()
        win:status("x = "..canvas.x)
    end
    sys.exit()
end)

waitall()

y.__description: Get or set the Canvas vertical position. The vertical position start from 0 (top side of the parent widget) and increase to the bottom side of the parent widget.
y.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("canvas.y sample", 600, 300)
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 140, 0)
ui.run(win)

async(function()
    while win.visible and (canvas.y < 282) do
        canvas.y = canvas.y + 2
        canvas:begin()
        canvas:clear(math.random(0xFFFFFFFF))
        canvas:flip()
        sleep(5)
        win:status("y = "..canvas.y)
    end
    sys.exit()
end)

waitall()

Canvas.__description: The Canvas widget draws shapes, text, and images on a window surface

begin.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple window
local win = ui.Window("canvas.onPaint() sample", "fixed", 500, 400)
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"

function canvas:onPaint()
    -- Alternates canvas fill using a random color...
    self:begin()
    self:clear(math.random(0xFFFFFFFF))
    self:flip()
    sleep(250)
    -- ...and a red color
    self:begin()
    self:clear(0xFF0000FF)
    self:flip()
    sleep(250)
end

ui.run(win):wait()
begin.__tinydesc: Prepares the Canvas back buffer for drawing

LinearGradient.__returns: This function returns an LinearGradient instance.
LinearGradient.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("LinearGradient.constructor() sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.bgcolor = 0x000000FF

canvas.fontsize = 37.5
canvas.fontstyle = "italic"
canvas.fontweight = 900

local gradient = canvas:LinearGradient { [0] = 0x00FFFFFF, [1] = 0x00008bFF }
gradient.start = { 0, 0 }
gradient.stop = { canvas.width, canvas.height }

local angle = 0

function canvas:onPaint()
  canvas:clear()
  canvas:rotate(angle)
  angle = angle + 2
  canvas:print("LuaRT Canvas", 150, 130, gradient)
end

-- update user interface
ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description:
A table where :

  • keys are a number : a relative gradient position (only positions between 0.0 to 1.0 are visible, but any other positions might impact the gradient).
  • values are a number : a color in RGBA format.

  • The color is represented by a number, an RGBA value (one byte per primary color and one byte for alpha channel).
  • A RGBA color can be represented as an hexadecimal number : 0xRRGGBBAA , RR meaning a 8bit hexadecimal red value, and so on.

LinearGradient.__description: Creates an LinearGradient object instance to be used in any Canvas drawing operation.
LinearGradient.__tinydesc: Creates a new linear gradient brush

scale.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

local win = ui.Window("Canvas - Scaling example", "fixed", 150, 150)

local c = ui.Canvas(win)
c.align = "all"

local scale = 0
local delta = 0.02

-- Uses internal timer for 30fps drawing
function c:onPaint()
    self:begin()
    self:clear(0xffffffff)
    -- Resets the canvas transformations
    c:identity()
    -- Sets the new scale transformation
    c:scale(scale, scale)
    c:fillrect(50,50,100,100, 0xF0A0D7FF)
    self:flip()
    sleep()
    scale = scale + delta
    if scale > 2 then
        delta = -0.02
    elseif scale < 0.02 then
        delta = 0.02
    end
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the x-axis scale factor.
A number representing the y-axis scale factor.
The horizonal coordinate for the scale center point, defaulting to the center of the Canvas.
The vertical coordinate for the scale center point, defaulting to the center of the Canvas.

scale.__description: Applyies a scale transformation on the Canvas

align.__description:
Get or set the Canvas alignment, which position and size will be aligned relative to its parent (alignment persists even if the size of the parent changes).

This property uses a string to specify the Canvas alignment :

  • "all" : the Canvas will be aligned along all the parent borders (and will cover the entire parent client area).
  • "bottom" : the Canvas will be aligned along the bottom border of the parent, preserving its height.
  • "top" : the Canvas will be aligned along the the top border of the parent, preserving its height.
  • "right" : the Canvas will be aligned along the right border of the parent, preserving its width.
  • "left" : the Canvas will be aligned along the left border of the parent, preserving its width.
align.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:align() sample", "fixed", 320, 200)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)

-- align the Canvas in all of its parent area 
canvas.align = "all"

-- set the onPaint() event handler to draw on the canvas
function canvas:onPaint()
    self:begin()
    self:clear()
    self:print("Hello World !", 10, 10)
    self:flip()
end

-- shows the Window and update ui asynchronously
ui.run(win):wait()
align.__tinydesc: Align the canvas relative to its parent

fillroundrect.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.fillroundrect() sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.bgcolor = 0x000000FF

local w = canvas.width
local h = canvas.height

function canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:fillroundrect(math.random(w), math.random(w), math.random(h), math.random(h), 6, 6, math.random(0xFFFFFFFF))
  self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the x coordinate of the upper left corner (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the y coordinate of the upper left corner (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
A number representing the x coordinate of the lower right corner
A number representing the y coordinate of the lower right corner
A number representing x-radius for the quarter ellipse that is drawn at each corner.
A number representing y-radius for the quarter ellipse that is drawn at each corner.

An optional value that can represent any of the following values :

  • A RGBA color, as an integer value
  • LinearGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored linear gradient
  • RadialGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored radial gradient
  • Image : The line will be drawn with a picture pattern.
When this parameter is omitted, the rounded rectangle is drawn using the current Canvas.color

fillroundrect.__description: Draws a filled and rounded rectangle on the Canvas

enabled.__description: Get or set the Canvas ability to respond to mouse, and any other events. When set to false, disable the Canvas (the user will not be able to interact with it), and Canvas's events won't be fired anymore.
enabled.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window 
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.enabled sample", 320, 200)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
Canvas.align = "all"

-- disable the Canvas
Canvas.enabled = false

-- onClick event won't be fired as the Canvas is disabled
function Canvas:onClick(x, y)
  ui.info("You have clicked on the Canvas at position "..x..", "..y)
end

ui.run(win):wait()

circle.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.rect() sample", "fixed", 500, 400)

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win)
canvas.align = "all"
canvas.bgcolor = 0x000000FF

function canvas:onPaint()
    self:begin()
    self:circle(math.random(w), math.random(h), math.random(150), math.random(0xFFFFFFFF), 5)
    self:flip()
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__params.__description: A number representing the x coordinate of the upper left corner (zero means the left side of the Canvas)
A number representing the y coordinate of the upper left corner (zero means the top side of the Canvas)
A number representing the radius of the circle

An optional value that can represent any of the following values :

  • A RGBA color, as an integer value
  • LinearGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored linear gradient
  • RadialGradient : The line will be drawn with a colored radial gradient
  • Image : The line will be drawn with a picture pattern.
When this parameter is omitted, the rectangle is drawn using the current Canvas.color
An optional number value representing the circle border thickness, defaulting to 1

onLeave.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a fixed Window
local win = ui.Window("Canvas:onLeave() event sample", "fixed", 320, 200)
win:status("")

-- create a Canvas
local canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 0, 100, 100)
canvas.cursor = "cross"
canvas.bgcolor = 0xFFA0A0FF
canvas:center()

function canvas:onLeave()
  win:status("You leaved the Canvas")
end

ui.run(win):wait()
onLeave.__description: This event is fired when the mouse cursor leaves the Canvas.
onLeave.__tinydesc: Event fired when the mouse cursor leaves the Canvas

hide.__example:
local ui = require "ui"
require "canvas"

-- create a simple Canvas 
local win = ui.Window("Canvas.visible sample", "fixed", 640, 480)
local Canvas = ui.Canvas(win, 0, 50, 640, 430)
Canvas.fontsize = 30

function Canvas:onPaint()
  self:begin()
  self:clear() 
  self:print("Hello", math.random(0,630), math.random(0, 390), math.random(0xFFFFFFFF))
  self:flip()
end

local button = ui.Button(win, "Hide the Canvas widget")
button:center()
button.y = 10

function button:onClick()
  Canvas.visible = not Canvas.visible
  button.text = (Canvas.visible and "Hide" or "Show").. " the Canvas widget"
end

ui.run(win):wait()

__tinydesc: Widget to draw shapes, text, and images on a graphical surface
